name: Warcraft
description: Zug zug — the peon barks, war cries, and villain lines that carried Warcraft from Azeroth's battlefields into gaming legend.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Orc Peon's weary reply when ordered to gather resources.

quotes.source: Warcraft II: Tides of Darkness, Blizzard Entertainment (1995)
quotes.notes: Orcish for rough agreement — the Peon's acknowledgment, a series-long catchphrase.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Human Peasant's line on finishing a task.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Human Peasant's selection response.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Orc Peon's selection line.

quotes.content: Me not that kind of orc!
quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: One of the Peon's joking repeated-click lines.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: Orcish for 'I obey' — the Grunt's order acknowledgment.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Human Peasant's exasperated reply after too many clicks.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: Orcish war cry meaning 'Victory or death,' the rallying motto of the Horde.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Horde's defining battle cry across the Warcraft series.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Human Footman's charge, naming the kingdom of Lordaeron.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Human Footman's selection response.

quotes.authorName: Illidan Stormrage (Warcraft III: The Frozen Throne)
quotes.source: Warcraft III: The Frozen Throne, Blizzard Entertainment (2003)
quotes.notes: Illidan's warning, later immortalized in the World of Warcraft: The Burning Crusade trailer.

quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The Death Knight Arthas speaking of the cursed runeblade Frostmourne.

quotes.source: World of Warcraft, Blizzard Entertainment (2004)
quotes.notes: The Goblin greeting that sums up their profit-driven outlook.

quotes.source: World of Warcraft, Blizzard Entertainment (2004)
quotes.notes: The candle-headed Kobold's frantic protest, a beloved early-game meme.

quotes.source: World of Warcraft, Blizzard Entertainment (2004)
quotes.notes: The gurgling battle cry of the Murlocs, an instantly recognizable Warcraft sound.

quotes.content: You are not my king yet, boy! Nor would I obey that command even if you were!
quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: Uther refusing Prince Arthas's order to purge Stratholme.

quotes.content: This kingdom shall fall, and from the ashes shall arise a new order that will shake the very foundations of the world.
quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: Arthas, corrupted by Frostmourne, returning to Lordaeron to kill his father.

quotes.content: Your journey has just begun, young prince. Gather your forces and meet me in Northrend.
quotes.source: Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos, Blizzard Entertainment (2002)
quotes.notes: The dreadlord Mal'Ganis luring Arthas toward his doom in the frozen north.
